channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: BLAST Rivals Fort Worth 2026 Tournament Guide—Schedule, Format, and Teams
item.content:encoded:
Tier 1 Counter-Strike turns its focus towards the United States of America as it prepares for its mini-NA swing with BLAST Rivals Fort Worth and IEM Atlanta in quick succession.

While IEM Atlanta will see Counter-Strike leave its traditional US heartland, BLAST Rivals Fort Worth takes place on familiar ground in Texas, where BLAST hosted its Austin Major in 2025.

The tournament will see eight teams, invited purely via VRS standings, compete for the BLAST Rivals trophy over a rapid-fire six days of CS action.

BLAST Rivals Fort Worth Schedule and Format

The BLAST Open Rotterdam will unfold towards the end of March, culminating in a grand final on March 29. Here’s the full schedule:

  • April 29-May 3
  • Group stage: April 29-30
  • Playoffs: May 1-3

The format is typical of BLAST eight-team events, with six of the eight teams advancing from groups to the playoffs. The winner of each four-team group will advance directly to the Semi-Finals.

Group Stage:

  • Two groups of four teams, double-elimination.
  • The top team from each group advances directly to the semi-finals.
  • Group runners-up and lower-bracket winners advance to the quarter-finals.
  • All matches are best-of-three.

Playoffs:

  • All matches are best-of-three, except the Grand Final, which is a best-of-five.
  • Six-team single-elimination bracket.

BLAST Rivals Fort Worth Teams

BLAST Rivals Fort Worth, like IEM Atlanta, faced several invitation rejections from the top teams. Falcons, Spirit, MOUZ, Aurora, Parivision, and The MongolZ all declined invitations, with Visa issues being a potential barrier.

Despite that, BLAST Fort Worth did attract several of the most-supported teams in Counter-Strike. The BLAST Rivals Fort Worth Teams are:

  • Vitality
  • FURIA
  • NAVI
  • Astralis
  • G2
  • FaZe
  • GamerLegion
  • FUT

The opening matchups have been revealed by BLAST. They are:

Group A:

  • Vitality vs FUT
  • Astralis vs G2

Group B:

  • NAVI vs FaZe
  • FURIA vs GamerLegion

Prize Pool:

BLAST Rivals Fort Worth features a $1,000,000 total prize pool, but its distribution is highly skewed towards Club Share, which is directly awarded to organizations.

  • Prize Pool: $1,000,000
  • Club Share: $650,000
  • First place: $250,000 ($125,000 of which is Club Share)

BLAST Rivals Fort Worth Location

BLAST Rivals Fort Worth will be played in full in Fort Worth, Texas. The playoffs will see fans attend the Dickies Arena in Forth Worth.

Unusually for BLAST, the stage will be in the centre of the arena as part of a 360 set-up.

How to Watch BLAST Rivals Fort Worth 2026

BLAST Open Rotterdam 2026 can be watched on BLAST Premier’s channels across platforms on Twitch, YouTube, and BLAST’s own platform:

  • BLAST Premier Twitch
  • BLAST Premier YouTube

BLAST Rivals Fort Worth 2026 Predictions

You know what they say? Counter-Strike is a game where 10 people enter the server, play between two and five maps, and in the end, Vitality wins.

Vitality are the unstoppable force at the top of Counter-Strike, and there is no immovable object to challenge them.

Truly, there is little hope of a shock upset at BLAST Rivals that deprives Vitality of another trophy, but there are still intriguing storylines to delve into elsewhere, like ‘who will come second?’

After a Semi-Final run at their home event at IEM Rio, Furia seem best-placed to challenge Vitality again.

The two played out a competitive series in Rio, but the pressure of delivering at Gabriel ‘FalleN⁠’ Toledo’s last Brazilian Tier 1 tournament seemed to wilt the team, especially young AWPer Danil ‘molodoy’ Golubenko.

The pressure will be less fierce in front of a largely nonpartisan NA crowd, but they also threaten to revert to their pre-IEM Rio form, which severely struggled on the T-side.

FUT returns to Tier 1 after their PGL Bucharest 2026 victory. The young roster was formerly the core of NAVI Junior, NAVI’s academy team.

With one trophy each this year, PGL Bucharest and ESL Pro League Season 23, respectively, FUT are on pace to challenge their once senior team, showing rapid and continuous progression against Tier 1 teams.

NAVI themselves have been making strides, but are generally helpless against Vitality, as displayed by their IEM Rio Quarter-Final elimination.

The team is playing more solid CS than at any time since StarLadder Budapest 2025, but still lacks star power despite Drin ‘makazze’ Shaqiri’s immense growth individually.

Astralis have also shown strong signs of growth, and start in an interesting matchup against G2, who showed positive signs at IEM Rio despite fielding a stand-in.

FaZe will start against NAVI in their first contest since the departure of Finn ‘karrigan’ Andersen to Falcons. Russel ‘Twistzz’ Van Dulken described the news as “sudden” to the roster.

In losing a core piece of FaZe’s identity, it is hard to see FaZe assert themselves so early after the loss of their captain.

FaZe are expected to field French rifler Ryan ‘Neityu’ Aubry in the meantime, with French leaker neL sharing that Twistzz will also take over IGLing duties in the interim.

Predicted winner: Team Vitality

Who do you think will finish second at BLAST Rivals Fort Worth? Discuss that on our Discord.
For more CS news, see why German player MAUschine slapped his opponent at a local LAN.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Valve Secretly Adds New Steam Controller Unboxing Video
item.content:encoded:
Everyone knows that Valve is currently developing the long-awaited Steam Machine, but a new, secret Steam Controller unboxing video has been uploaded to the Steam storefront, which is sure to be a needed accessory for the console.

Steam has held dominion over much of gaming for years, and it’s showing its hand even further with the upcoming Steam Machine. As with all consoles, expect a flurry of handy accessories and devices designed to complement your new Steam machine, one of which is sure to be the new Steam Controller.

Steam Controller Unboxing Video Discovered on Steam

I’ll preface by saying that—as of writing—the video is unavailable to watch right now, so you will have to wait a bit longer to see the new controller in action.

However, the ‘steam_controller_unboxing_2026’ ID exists on Steam, and its last update was on April 20 (again, at the time of writing), as shown by Brad Lynch and various other social media posts.

It’s unclear how the long video is going to be, or if there will be any information tied to it, such as pricing or release date, but this does all suggest that the actual video must be arriving soon.

If it does, it also makes us wonder if we’re about to get new Steam Machine information to go along with it. One of the most recent updates was that the Steam Machine will target 1080p at 30 FPS for Steam Deck-verified games.

A release window or pricing would be a much-needed update, considering Linus Tech Tips thinks the Steam Machine price will be around $700!

Are you looking forward to the new Steam Controller and the Steam Machine? Let us know what you think of all of this through the Insider Gaming Discord.

Check out Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced footage leaking online and PlayStation requiring age verification for certain online features, for more gaming news.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: Animgraph 2 goes Live in Counter-Strike 2: What Can Players Expect?
item.content:encoded:
Valve has pushed the Animgraph 2 animation system to live in Counter-Strike 2, drastically altering animations within the tactical first-person shooter.

The animation overhaul follows a two-week public beta test of Animgraph 2, which attempted to root out bugs and fix them before the system was pushed to the live game.

However, that beta test had no officially supported servers, so most players are only getting a hands-on experience against other players now.

Animgraph 2 full release: What has changed?

What players should now be experiencing is a series of benefits of Valve’s new animation software, which replaces the AnimGraph system initially deployed for CS2.

Benefits include:

  • More readable model animations
  • Reduced packet size for animation data
  • Less CPU load
  • Smoother animations on sloped surfaces, and when crouch-jumping

Together, the benefits should lead to a more responsive and intuitive set of animations for players, due to the animations themselves and the reduced load of calculating them.

The more readable animations have seemingly reduced peeker’s advantage, and increased players’ abilities to hold angles more comfortable.

Overall, the changes bring the game more visually in line with CSGO, but also mechanically closer with players seemingly having an easier time holding angles.

Players have also reported a drastic increase in their game’s performance after the update, in some cases gaining approximately 15% frames per second. As CS2 is traditionally CPU-bottlenecked, the reduced workload may provide significant dividends depending on your machine.

The update also brings with it some third-person animations for players to flex their knives and skins. The game now shows bespoke knife pullout animations for specific knives.

Some weapon pullout animations have also been altered, but players will have to wait for their inspect animations to be reflected in third-person.

Burst fire bug in CS2 update quickly patched

While the beta test served to remove several bugs in the model, the Animgraph 2 launch was not flawless, suffering from one short-lived bug that made its way into Premier and Competitive matchmaking.

Weapons with burst fire (Glock/FAMAS) were bugged upon the release of the system, making all three bursted bullets 100% accurate to the same spot simultaneously. The update temporarily made the weapons the most powerful guns in the game before quickly being patched out.

Counter-Strike 2 Animgraph 2 patch notes in full:

Animgraph2

  • Minor adjustments to viewmodel animations.
  • Adjusted general weapon deploy animation logic.
  • Fixed issues with transitioning between knife attacks.
  • Fixed Elites not shooting in third-person.

Misc

  • Fixed a bug that allowed silently climbing ladders at run speed by sporadically tapping movement keys.
  • Adjusted ground smoothing at locations where sloped ground surfaces transition to flat ground.
  • Fixed held grenades inheriting incorrect scale in some circumstances such as after being dropped and picked up.
  • Fixed a crash at halftime when transitioning from CT to T.

Second update (Burst fire bug fix)

  • Fixed a bug that removed the delay between burst fire bullets.

What do you think of the update? Let us know on Insider Gaming’s Discord!
For more CS2 news, see the news of karrigan’s move to Team Falcons.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson
